header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary:
I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Organizational Development professional who thrives on building scalable learning programs, establishing metrics that demonstrate impact, and partnering across teams to enable people. 
Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve people development—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Upskill HR team in {data|data analytics} to audit 300+ leave cases, successfully refuting cost assumptions via {data|evidence-based insights}
Empower HR to present executive-ready insights on $362K in leave costs, leading to {prog|policy redesigns and first aid training}
Conduct needs assessments with 25+ {cross|stakeholders} to {prog|identify learning gaps} and design 3 {prog|enablement programs} for product launch
bullets.original: Conduct needs assessments with 25+ global stakeholders to identify learning gaps and design 3 new educational programs
bullets.reason: Swapped 'educational programs' → 'enablement programs for product launch' to mirror Mercury's CS enablement framing

bullets.reason: Surfaces 'scalable knowledge systems' + 'onboarding documentation' to map JD's scalable learning systems and KM practices

bullets.reason: Adds 'end-to-end' + 'content audits' to mirror JD's end-to-end program ownership and training-material auditing

bullets.reason: Moved up; reordered list to lead with L&D for visibility in this role's emphasis

jd.meta: Mercury — Customer Support Learning & Enablement Specialist · SF / NYC / Portland OR / Remote (US/Canada) · $95,600–$132,800 (US) · CS Operations

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Designs and scales {prog|learning initiatives} across Mercury's Customer Support function. Owns {coord|end-to-end enablement programs}, defines timelines, drives coordination, establishes {data|learning metrics}, creates {prog|engaging instructional content}, develops {coord|scalable learning systems}, improves {coord|knowledge management practices}, and audits training materials. Partners with {cross|CS, Quality, and Strategy} to identify performance gaps and deliver targeted learning solutions.

items.text: Manage {coord|end-to-end enablement programs} — define timelines and drive coordination
Establish {data|learning metrics} to measure training effectiveness
Create engaging {prog|instructional content} across multiple formats
Partner with {cross|CS, Quality, Strategy} to identify performance gaps
3+ years in L&D, instructional design, or enablement (ideally CS / regulated environments) ✅
Demonstrated end-to-end program ownership with stakeholder + timeline management ✅
Data-informed approach with experience defining success metrics ✅
Strong instructional design and facilitation capabilities ⚠️ (facilitation strong; ID limited)
Familiarity with LMS platforms (WorkRamp, Articulate) ⚠️ (Canvas/Moodle yes; Articulate no)

sections.title: ✅ H1B Status
sections.content: Mercury Technologies Inc has confirmed LCA filings on h1bdata.info (mostly SF Senior Software Engineer roles). Sponsorship infrastructure exists; non-tech sponsorship not directly verified. Strong sponsor track record.

changeLog.change: Reframed L&D identity: 'building scalable learning programs', 'establishing metrics that demonstrate impact', 'partnering across teams to enable people' — directly maps Mercury's three pillars (program ownership, metrics, cross-functional).
Kept Pair A 'Upskill/Empower' bullets — directly demonstrates internal-team training and data-driven training outcomes per L&D playbook.

Bullet 1: 'enablement programs for product launch' — surfaces JD's enablement framing. Bullet 2: 'scalable knowledge systems' + 'onboarding documentation' — KM + scalable systems. Bullet 3: 'engagement and completion metrics' — explicit learning metrics. Bullet 4: 'end-to-end' + 'content audits' — program ownership + training audits.
Lead with internal training consultations (most L&D-adjacent), then L&D-focused cross-functional projects, then data/manager work.
Lead reframed to 'design and pilot... scaled to 23 locations' — directly maps to scalable learning systems. Onboarding moved up (KM parallel).
Lead with customer-service/communication bullet — bridge to Mercury's customer support context. Then events facilitation, JD drafting, broad HR last.

Led with LMS (Canvas, Moodle) per JD. Added Knowledge Management (Notion, SharePoint, Google Workspace) for JD's KM emphasis. Kept Design Tools (Canva, Adobe InDesign) honest — did NOT claim Articulate / WorkRamp since Jamie has no hands-on experience.

Two acknowledged gaps: (1) Articulate / WorkRamp specifically — listed Canvas/Moodle truthfully. (2) Pure instructional design depth — Jamie's strength is program ownership + facilitation, not authoring. Address in cover letter / interview, do not overclaim in resume.
